The etymology of the Ukrainian village "Горохівськe" traces back to the word "горох" (horokh), which means "pea" in Ukrainian. The village name is derived from the fact that it was likely originally founded or known for its cultivation of peas or a pea-like crop.

As for the vocabulary of the "Горохівськe" dialect of Ukrainian, it may include variations or unique words and phrases developed and used by the local population of the village or region.
